title: Decode protobuf LiftLog backup to JSON in refresh workflow
description: The .gz files in s3://liftlog-backups/ decompress to protobuf binary, not JSON. The refresh-backup.yml workflow currently writes raw binary to data/backup.json which breaks the v2 page. Need to add a protobuf decode step — either find the LiftLog .proto schema and use protoc/buf, reverse-engineer from the existing data shape, or use a Python script that reads the known structure. The v2 page currently displays the hardcoded Phase 4 prototype data rather than real backup data.

title: Automate AWS backup refresh and GitHub Pages publish
description: Run a scheduled GitHub Action on GitHub-hosted runners that checks personal AWS storage for the newest LiftLog backup, downloads it only when newer, decompresses the .gz, updates the latest.json pointer, and deploys the static site to GitHub Pages.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A scheduled workflow runs automatically.
- [ ] The workflow checks AWS for the newest backup.
- [ ] The workflow skips publishing when the backup has not changed.
- [ ] The workflow decompresses the backup and publishes the site assets.
- [ ] The workflow updates the published pointer file used by the page.
notes: Source: migrated from GitHub issue #4 in cannontrodder/liftlog-hardcopy. GitHub issue closed after migration.

title: Load latest backup and choose the next workout by age
description: Load the published backup via the pointer file, derive the current workout from the latest backup snapshot, and default to the workout type whose last completed timestamp is oldest. Keep the visible history to the last 4 sessions.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The page reads the current backup location from a lightweight pointer file.
- [ ] The page renders the latest workout data entirely client side.
- [ ] The default workout is chosen by oldest last-completed timestamp.
- [ ] The last 4 sessions are visible in the workout history.
notes: Source: migrated from GitHub issue #1 in cannontrodder/liftlog-hardcopy. GitHub issue closed after migration.

title: LiftLog hard copy v2
description: Consolidated follow-up for the client-side LiftLog hard-copy site. This epic covers loading the latest published backup, selecting the next workout by age, persisting in-progress reps locally, showing partial completion state, and automating the AWS-to-GitHub Pages publish loop.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The site loads the latest published backup through a pointer file.
- [ ] The next workout is chosen by oldest last-completed timestamp.
- [ ] In-progress reps persist locally.
- [ ] Exercise progress can be complete, partial, or not started.
- [ ] A scheduled GitHub Action refreshes and publishes the static site.
notes: Source: migrated from GitHub issue set in cannontrodder/liftlog-hardcopy. The GitHub issues were closed after the move to local beads.

title: Persist workout set entries in local storage
description: Persist in-progress set entry data locally so reps survive refresh and return visits during the current workout. This is client-only state, no server round-trip.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] Entering reps writes the draft state to local storage.\n- [ ] Reloading the page restores saved draft values.\n- [ ] Persistence is scoped to the current workout data in this prototype.
notes: Source: migrated from GitHub issue #2 in cannontrodder/liftlog-hardcopy. GitHub issue closed after migration.

title: Show completion state in the workout breadcrumb
description: Extend the breadcrumb / exercise position indicator so it reflects complete, partial, and not started states when the user moves around out of order. The indicator should show saved progress after reload.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] Exercises can be shown as complete, partial, or not started.\n- [ ] The breadcrumb indicator updates when the user leaves an exercise unfinished.\n- [ ] The indicator matches the saved per-exercise progress state after refresh.
notes: Source: migrated from GitHub issue #3 in cannontrodder/liftlog-hardcopy. GitHub issue closed after migration.
